metadata.archetype_justification: The application owns persistent state via TypeORM entities (20 entities with CRUD operations), exposes a full REST API surface with create/update/delete endpoints, and manages user-specific data (API keys, credentials, workspaces).
metadata.context: Low-code UI for building LLM flows and agents.

top_gaps.title: Managed Compute
top_gaps.gap: No managed compute — Docker Compose only, no ECS/EKS/Lambda
top_gaps.impact: Blocks container orchestration, auto-scaling, and HA

top_gaps.title: Infrastructure as Code Coverage
top_gaps.gap: No IaC — zero Terraform, CDK, or CloudFormation
top_gaps.impact: Cannot reproduce environments, no disaster recovery

top_gaps.title: Audit Logging
top_gaps.gap: No CloudTrail or equivalent audit logging in IaC
top_gaps.impact: No forensic analysis capability, compliance gap

top_gaps.title: Deployment Strategy
top_gaps.gap: No blue/green or canary — direct-to-production via manual dispatch

top_gaps.title: Network Security
top_gaps.gap: No VPC, security groups, or network segmentation defined

quick_agent_wins.name: API-Aware Agent
quick_agent_wins.prerequisite: API docs exist (APP-Q5 = 2) and structured JSON responses detected via Swagger/OpenAPI spec
quick_agent_wins.what_it_enables: An agent that discovers and invokes Flowise API endpoints as tools for natural language orchestration
quick_agent_wins.additional_steps: The Swagger spec needs to be fully populated; all 56 route modules should have OpenAPI annotations

quick_agent_wins.name: RAG-Based Knowledge Agent
quick_agent_wins.prerequisite: Documentation exists in repository — README, i18n docs, SECURITY.md, extensive .env.example documentation
quick_agent_wins.what_it_enables: A knowledge agent that indexes Flowise documentation and provides developer assistance
quick_agent_wins.additional_steps: Consolidate documentation into a structured corpus

quick_agent_wins.name: Observability Agent
quick_agent_wins.prerequisite: Structured logging and tracing in place (OPS-Q1 = 3) with Winston JSON logging and OpenTelemetry traces
quick_agent_wins.what_it_enables: An agent that queries logs, traces incidents, and suggests root causes for failed predictions
quick_agent_wins.additional_steps: Ensure OTel trace propagation covers BullMQ worker flows

findings.title: No Managed Compute
findings.description: All compute runs as Docker containers orchestrated by Docker Compose. No ECS, EKS, Lambda, or Fargate resources are defined.
findings.gap: No managed container orchestration or serverless compute. Docker Compose provides no auto-scaling, no self-healing, and no managed deployment lifecycle.
findings.recommendation: Deploy on EKS with separate Deployments for the main server and BullMQ worker. Use Fargate for burstable worker pods. Define Helm charts for both workloads.

findings.title: Self-Managed Databases
findings.description: Databases are self-managed via Docker Compose or local SQLite. No managed database infrastructure (RDS, Aurora, DynamoDB) is defined in IaC.
findings.gap: All databases are self-managed with no automated failover, backup, or scaling.
findings.recommendation: Migrate to Aurora PostgreSQL for the primary database with Multi-AZ failover. Use ElastiCache for Redis.

findings.title: Basic Workflow Orchestration
findings.description: BullMQ provides task queue orchestration but multi-step workflows are hardcoded as sequential application logic.
findings.gap: Multi-step workflows are hardcoded in BullMQ job handlers with no visual management or cross-service state tracking.
findings.recommendation: Evaluate AWS Step Functions for document processing and chatflow execution workflows.

findings.title: Self-Managed Messaging Infrastructure
findings.description: BullMQ uses self-managed Redis. No managed messaging service (SQS, SNS, EventBridge) is in use.
findings.gap: Self-managed Redis for messaging. Redis failure would take down the queue system without managed failover.
findings.recommendation: Migrate to ElastiCache for Redis. Introduce EventBridge for event-driven patterns.

findings.title: No Network Security
findings.description: No VPC, subnet, security group, or network segmentation is defined. Docker Compose exposes port 3000 directly.
findings.gap: No network segmentation. Services are not deployed in private subnets with least-privilege security groups.
findings.recommendation: Define a VPC with private subnets for EKS worker nodes, Aurora, and ElastiCache. Use VPC endpoints for S3 and Secrets Manager.

findings.title: No Managed API Entry Point
findings.description: The Express server is exposed directly on port 3000 without API Gateway, ALB, or CloudFront.
findings.gap: No managed entry point with throttling, request validation, and WAF.
findings.recommendation: Deploy API Gateway in front of the EKS service with throttling and WAF. Use CloudFront for the React UI.

findings.title: No Auto-Scaling
findings.description: Docker Compose runs single instances with no scaling mechanism. No HPA, ASG, or auto-scaling configuration exists.
findings.gap: All capacity is statically provisioned. Cannot respond to traffic spikes.
findings.recommendation: Configure HPA on EKS for both server and worker deployments. Configure Aurora auto-scaling for read replicas.

findings.title: No Backup and Recovery
findings.description: No backup configuration exists. No automated backups, no PITR, no restore procedures.
findings.gap: No automated backups for any data store. Data loss would be unrecoverable.
findings.recommendation: Aurora PostgreSQL provides automated backups and PITR. Enable S3 versioning. Define an AWS Backup plan.

findings.title: No Multi-AZ Deployment
findings.description: Single-instance Docker Compose deployment. Architecture supports multi-AZ but no HA configuration is defined.
findings.gap: No multi-AZ deployment. Single point of failure for all components.
findings.recommendation: Deploy on EKS across 2+ AZs. Use Aurora Multi-AZ. Use ElastiCache Multi-AZ.

findings.title: No Infrastructure as Code
findings.description: Zero infrastructure is defined in IaC. No Terraform, CDK, CloudFormation, Helm charts, or Kustomize.
findings.gap: 0% IaC coverage. Infrastructure changes are manual, non-reproducible, and undocumented.
findings.recommendation: Create Terraform modules defining VPC, EKS, Aurora, ElastiCache, S3, API Gateway, IAM, and CloudWatch alarms.

findings.title: Partial CI/CD Automation
findings.description: GitHub Actions provides automated build/test but deployment is manual dispatch only.
findings.gap: Build is automated but deployment is manual. No continuous delivery pipeline.
findings.recommendation: Extend with automated deployment stages. Implement ArgoCD for GitOps on EKS.

findings.title: Monolith with Identifiable Modules
findings.description: Single deployable unit with shared database schema. Clear package boundaries but no independent deployment or scaling.
findings.gap: All controllers access shared TypeORM entities. No independent scaling of sub-domains.
findings.recommendation: Apply Strangler Fig pattern to extract prediction engine, document store, and credential vault.

findings.title: Primarily Synchronous Communication
findings.description: All API endpoints respond synchronously. BullMQ async is optional and intra-application.
findings.gap: No event-driven communication between components. No EventBridge or managed messaging.
findings.recommendation: Introduce EventBridge for cross-service state propagation when services are extracted.

findings.title: Long-Running Process Handling Partially Async
findings.description: BullMQ handles long-running operations but some prediction paths are still synchronous when not in queue mode.
findings.gap: Default mode is non-queue (synchronous predictions). Status polling not universally applied.
findings.recommendation: Default all prediction paths to async. Expose standardized job status API.

findings.title: Single API Version Without Evolution Strategy
findings.description: API uses /api/v1/ prefix but only one version exists with no deprecation policy.
findings.gap: No backward compatibility guarantees or migration path for breaking changes.
findings.recommendation: Document a versioning policy. Use API Gateway versioning stages for production.

findings.title: No Service Discovery
findings.description: All service endpoints are configured via static environment variables. No dynamic discovery.
findings.gap: All endpoints hard-coded in env vars. No Kubernetes DNS, Cloud Map, or service registry.
findings.recommendation: Leverage Kubernetes DNS on EKS. Use AWS Cloud Map if extracting to microservices.

findings.title: Unstructured Data on Local Filesystem by Default
findings.description: S3 is supported but default is local filesystem. No managed parsing pipeline (Textract).
findings.gap: Default is local filesystem storage. No managed parsing pipeline.
findings.recommendation: Default to S3 in production. Evaluate Textract for document parsing.

findings.title: Mostly Centralized Data Access
findings.description: TypeORM provides centralized data access. Some direct queries and vector store connections outside the main layer.
findings.gap: Components package has independent database connections for vector stores.
findings.recommendation: Standardize connection management for vector store backends.

findings.title: No Audit Logging
findings.description: No CloudTrail or infrastructure-level audit logging. Only application-level Winston request logs.
findings.gap: No infrastructure audit trail. No immutable log storage. No forensic analysis capability.
findings.recommendation: Enable CloudTrail with log file validation and S3 Object Lock. Define in IaC.

findings.title: No Infrastructure Encryption at Rest
findings.description: Application-level crypto-js encryption exists but no KMS, S3 bucket encryption, or RDS encryption.
findings.gap: No KMS. No S3 encryption policy. No database encryption at rest.
findings.recommendation: Configure customer-managed KMS keys for Aurora, S3, and ElastiCache.

findings.title: Application-Level Auth Without Gateway Defense
findings.description: JWT auth with Passport.js exists but no API Gateway authorizer for defense-in-depth.
findings.gap: No API Gateway authorizer. Public endpoints rely solely on application-level rate limiting.
findings.recommendation: Configure API Gateway JWT authorizers and WAF rules.

findings.title: Default Auth is Self-Managed
findings.description: External IdPs supported (Auth0, Google, OIDC) but default path is local username/password.
findings.gap: Primary auth is self-managed. No Cognito integration.
findings.recommendation: Consider Cognito User Pool as the centralized identity provider for AWS deployment.

findings.title: Most Secrets in Environment Variables
findings.description: Only encryption master key supports Secrets Manager. Database, Redis, S3 credentials are plain env vars.
findings.gap: Most production credentials in env vars without rotation.
findings.recommendation: Migrate all secrets to Secrets Manager with rotation. Use IAM roles for S3.

findings.title: No Compute Hardening
findings.description: Single-stage Dockerfile with build tools in production image. No vulnerability scanning.
findings.gap: No vulnerability scanning. Build tools and chromium in production image.
findings.recommendation: Use multi-stage builds. Enable ECR scanning. Integrate Trivy in CI.

findings.title: No Security Scanning in Pipeline
findings.description: CI runs lint and tests but no SAST, dependency scanning, or container scanning.
findings.gap: No Dependabot, npm audit, SAST, or container image scanning in CI.
findings.recommendation: Add npm audit, Dependabot, Semgrep, and ECR image scanning.

findings.title: Tracing Gaps in Worker Flows
findings.description: OpenTelemetry integrated on primary server. Unclear if trace context propagates through BullMQ.
findings.gap: Trace propagation through queue boundaries not confirmed. OTel exports to Datadog only.
findings.recommendation: Ensure trace propagation through BullMQ. Add X-Ray exporter to OTel Collector.

findings.title: No SLO Definitions
findings.description: No SLO definitions, error budgets, or latency targets defined.
findings.gap: Cannot measure whether the system is meeting user expectations.
findings.recommendation: Define SLOs for prediction latency, chatflow success rate, and document processing completion.

findings.title: Operational Metrics Only
findings.description: Prometheus counters and PostHog analytics exist but no formal business outcome metrics.
findings.gap: No business outcome metrics (cost-per-prediction, time-to-value) in observability stack.
findings.recommendation: Define and publish business metrics to CloudWatch custom metrics.

findings.title: No Alerting Configured
findings.description: Metrics collected but no alerting rules or anomaly detection configured.
findings.gap: No automated response to anomalies. Dashboards only.
findings.recommendation: Configure CloudWatch anomaly detection. Define Grafana alerting rules.

findings.title: No Deployment Strategy
findings.description: Manual dispatch to ECR. No blue/green, canary, or staged deployment. No rollback mechanism.
findings.gap: Direct-to-production with no staged rollout or automated rollback.
findings.recommendation: Implement ArgoCD on EKS with Argo Rollouts for canary deployments.

findings.title: Limited Integration Testing
findings.description: Unit tests exist. Cypress E2E covers only 2 basic flows. No integration tests for AI workflows.
findings.gap: Missing integration tests for prediction, document processing, and multi-agent execution.
findings.recommendation: Add integration tests for critical AI workflows. Use Testcontainers for PostgreSQL/Redis.

findings.title: No Incident Response Automation
findings.description: No runbooks, no automated remediation, no self-healing patterns.
findings.gap: Incident response is entirely ad hoc.
findings.recommendation: Create runbooks for common incidents. Implement SSM Automation for remediation.

findings.title: No Observability Ownership
findings.description: Dashboards exist but no CODEOWNERS, no named alarm owners, no team attribution.
findings.gap: No ownership attribution on observability assets.
findings.recommendation: Create CODEOWNERS. Add team tags to dashboards and alarms.

findings.title: No Resource Tagging
findings.description: No IaC means no resource tagging governance.
findings.gap: No tags on any AWS resources. No cost allocation or ownership attribution.
findings.recommendation: Define mandatory tags in IaC: Environment, Service, Team, CostCenter, ManagedBy.

triggering_questions.note: Monolith with identifiable modules but shared database schema

immediate_actions: Deploy monolith on EKS as Phase 1
Extract prediction engine as first service

pathways.name: Move to Containers
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q1 < 3 AND no container definitions — but Dockerfiles exist (contextual guard blocks)

triggering_questions.note: Score meets threshold but contextual guard blocks: Dockerfiles and Docker Compose already exist

pathways.not_triggered_reason: Compute is already containerized via Docker. Dockerfiles and Docker Compose are present. The pathway targets VM-to-container migration which is not applicable here.

pathways.name: Move to Open Source
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: DATA-Q4 < 3 AND commercial DB engines detected — neither condition met

triggering_questions.note: No stored procedures; all logic in application layer. Threshold not met.

pathways.not_triggered_reason: No commercial database engines detected. Application uses PostgreSQL, MySQL, MariaDB, SQLite — all open source.

triggering_questions.note: All databases self-managed via Docker Compose or local file

pathways.name: Move to Managed Analytics
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q4 < 3 AND evidence of data processing workloads — contextual guard: no analytics workloads detected

triggering_questions.note: Score meets threshold but no data processing/analytics workloads exist. BullMQ is a task queue, not an analytics pipeline.

pathways.not_triggered_reason: No data processing, ETL, or analytics workloads detected. BullMQ handles task execution (LLM predictions, document processing) not data analytics.

pathways.name: Move to Modern DevOps
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q10 < 3 OR INF-Q11 < 3 OR OPS-Q5 < 3 OR OPS-Q6 < 3

pathways.name: Move to AI
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: No AI/agent frameworks detected — but extensive AI frameworks ARE present (LangChain, Bedrock, OpenAI, etc.)
pathways.not_triggered_reason: AI/agent frameworks are extensively present. This IS an AI-native platform with 30+ LLM providers, 25+ vector databases, LangChain/LangGraph/LlamaIndex, and MCP protocol support. The Move to AI pathway targets systems that lack AI capabilities — not applicable here.
